old_claim.note: Liquid Crystal Displays (LCD Screens) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The early touch-display lineage was tied to computer display hardware rather than later LCD touch panels.

source_shape.source_locator: Johnson's 1965 paper frames the technology as a touch display input/output device for computers before later LCD touch-panel products.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports display hardware as a direct substrate but does not require LCD screens.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: CRT displays are the historically appropriate neighboring display lineage for mid-1960s computer display work; LCDs are a later touchscreen product substrate.

ontology_before: Touchscreen Interfaces depended on LCD screens, implying later flat-panel displays were prerequisites for a 1965 touch-display claim.
ontology_after: Touchscreen Interfaces depends on CRT Displays and Electronics for the early touch-display scope.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: liquid_crystal_displays_lcd_screens is replaced by cathode_ray_tube_crt_displays.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to consumer flat-panel touchscreens.
A dedicated computer_display_terminals node is added and should replace CRT displays.
A stronger source shows Johnson's first implementation did not use electronic display hardware.
short_reason: LCD is too late for the 1965 touch-display claim.
